description_long: This is a traditional wooden windmill with a tall, rectangular main structure elevated on sturdy wooden stilts. The windmill features four large lattice-style blades attached to a central hub, radiating outward and dominating the visual profile. A wooden staircase and platform lead up to a small entry door, suggesting access for maintenance or operation. The roof is steeply pitched and covered in dark shingles, while the body is clad in vertical wooden planks. The base is surrounded by a simple, grassy terrain with some stonework visible near the foundation. The overall appearance is rustic and functional, typical of historical windmills found in rural or park settings.
description: A tall, rustic wooden windmill with four large lattice blades, elevated on stilts and accessed by a wooden staircase. The structure features a steep shingled roof and vertical plank siding.
description_view_0: Front view showing the entry staircase, platform, and the full face of the windmill with all four blades visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the structure, the pitched roof, and the profile of the blades.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back wall, the supporting stilts, and the symmetrical arrangement of the blades.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the wooden siding, the staircase from a different angle, and the windmill's elevated base.
